decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed due to exceeding the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material property is already at its maximum effective value, the next most impactful lever is to increase the physical size of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat distribution. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.7 to 0.84), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the area available for lateral heat conduction away from the chip, reducing the peak temperature gradient. This change is within the 1.5x step limit and respects the design domain width of 1.2.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Improved feasibility of the thermal design
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the left/right Dirichlet boundaries; ensure it does not touch them to avoid singularities or unintended short-circuiting of the thermal path if boundary logic changes.
If width increase alone is insufficient, subsequent steps may need to adjust spreader height or base material conductivity.
